steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Cross-Text Connections question. Text 1 makes an underlined claim; you must find how the Text 2 researchers would respond to it. The claim in Text 1 is that emotional contagion requires physical interaction and observation of body language. Text 2 found emotional contagion occurring through text-based social media posts — no physical interaction at all.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It perpetuates a flawed understanding of emotional contagion, because there isn't enough evidence to suggest that smiling is a sign of emotional contagion.': Text 2 says nothing about smiling; smiling is only mentioned in Text 1 as supporting evidence. The Text 2 researchers would not object on those grounds. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It reflects an incomplete view of emotional contagion, because this phenomenon can occur even without in-person interaction.': This matches exactly. Yang and Ferrara found emotional contagion through text-based posts — proving physical interaction is not required — so Text 1's claim is incomplete, not wrong in every case. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It's fairly persuasive, because studies attempting to identify emotional contagion in situations without in-person interaction have thus far yielded unclear results.': Text 2 found clear results (positive posts led to more positive posts), so this misstates the study's outcome. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It's mostly accurate, because the social networking study confirmed that emotional contagion primarily occurs in response to negative emotions like nervousness.': Neither text claims emotional contagion is primarily negative; Text 2 shows it works for both positive and negative emotions. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
